title: Huperzia porophila
taxonomy: Huperzia porophila is a species of plant in the family Huperziaceae. It is a type of liverwort, a non-vascular land plant. The Huperziaceae family is part of the larger division of liverworts, which is the Marchantiophyta. Huperzia porophila is native to New Zealand. The classification of Huperzia porophila reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history among liverworts.

intro: Huperzia porophila, also known as a liverwort, is a type of non-vascular plant belonging to the family Huperziaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including its ability to enhance cognitive function and memory, as well as its possible neuroprotective properties. The bioactive constituents of Huperzia porophila include huperzine A, a potent inhibitor of acetylcholinesterase, as well as other alkaloids and flavonoids that contribute to its medicinal properties. Huperzia porophila is commonly used to prepare extracts and supplements, often in the form of capsules or tablets, which are marketed for their potential to support cognitive health and improve memory.
